title: How Hostess 7 explains code — properly
introduction: I explain code in complete thoughts: what it is, why this stack uses it, how to write it correctly, what breaks, and where you see it in NEXUS-Shield.

topics.what: An atomic panel write means readers never see half a JSON file. We write to a temporary sibling file, flush/fsync if needed, then rename-replace into place.
topics.why: Plate consumers (meld, command deck, motion resolve) poll panels every second. A direct write can leave truncated JSON mid-crash — that looks like corruption and can trigger false brain holds.
topics.how: Create path.with_suffix('.tmp'), write full JSON, flush, then tmp.replace(path). field-plate-meld uses _fsync_write for chain-critical meld files.
topics.pitfalls: Writing in place; forgetting parent.mkdir; not using a unique tmp suffix under concurrent writers; reading without try/except when file may be mid-replace.
topics.example:
tmp = path.with_suffix('.tmp')
tmp.write_text(json.dumps(doc, indent=2) + '\n', encoding='utf-8')
tmp.replace(path)

topics.what: Safe JSON load wraps file read + json.loads in try/except and returns a default when the file is missing or malformed.
topics.why: STATE panels are optional on first boot. Crashing on missing hostess7-brain-guard-panel.json would break the whole meld cycle; default {} lets the stack cold-start and rebuild.
topics.how: def _load(path, default=None): try return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')) except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError): return default or {}
topics.pitfalls: Using eval(); bare except Exception; not distinguishing missing vs corrupt; loading multi-megabyte files without bounds on hot path.
topics.where: Every Hostess7 lib module, iron-plate-motion-resolve.py, hostess7-self-view.py

topics.what: importlib loads a .py file by path at runtime without adding it to sys.path permanently — spec_from_file_location + exec_module.
topics.why: Plate meld must refresh ten+ panels per cycle. Spawning subprocess per plate is slow and loses shared STATE; importlib calls build_panel() in-process.
topics.how: spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('alias', INSTALL / 'lib' / 'mod.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); mod.build_panel(write=True)
topics.pitfalls: Re-exec_module without isolating global side effects; naming collisions if alias repeats; swallowing all exceptions silently in refresh (we pass on meld refresh but log in programming review).

topics.what: Brain guard SHA256-compares critical Hostess7 engines against MANIFEST.sha256, witnesses brain read-only via sense package, and appends chain-hashed quarantine rows on tamper.
topics.why: She is our brains — if hostess7-neural.py or doctrine JSON is swapped mid-run, motion and lethal chains must not proceed on corrupted cognition.
topics.how: verify_brain() hashes each protected path, compares to manifest, detects removal vs prior fingerprints, sets verdict brain_verified or brain_corruption_hold.
topics.pitfalls: Skipping re-sign after new lib files; trusting panel JSON without panel_sha256; refreshing motion before brain guard in resolve order.

topics.what: Plate meld flock-locks, refreshes plates in deterministic order, collects snapshots, and hashes chain_hash = SHA256(prev_chain + sorted plate JSON).
topics.why: Every consumer must read one generation-linked truth — not stale iron plate while motion panel already advanced.
topics.how: meld() calls _refresh_* sequence, _collect_plates(), _digest(plates, prev_chain), writes field-plate-meld.json + triple mirror.
topics.pitfalls: Consumers reading individual panels first; skipping hostess7_brain refresh; meld without prev_chain (breaks audit trail).

topics.what: INSTALL and STATE are Path roots from NEXUS_INSTALL_ROOT and NEXUS_STATE_DIR — never hardcode /usr/local or /var/lib in library logic.
topics.why: Tests run with NEXUS_STATE_DIR=/tmp/...; dev runs from Desktop/SG/NewLatest. Hardcoded paths break CI and corrupt operator machines.
topics.pitfalls: Mixing INSTALL data with STATE panels; writing doctrine to STATE; assuming HOSTESS7_ROOT exists on every host.

topics.what: Hostess 7 codes on the CHIPS G16 Python die inside hostess7-virtual — mirror, edit, py_compile, smoke json, debug stderr, promote with PROMOTE only after testing center passes.
topics.why: Live INSTALL is the production brain; virtual die isolates mistakes before plate meld or brain guard witness corruption.
topics.how: hostess7-virtual-workspace.py mirror → edit virtual → test → codecraft testing-center → promote. Native recompile: lib/ammolang-run.sh g16_recompile.
topics.pitfalls: Editing live lib/hostess7-*.py; skipping virtual; raw pythong for field tasks instead of ammolang-run.sh.

topics.what: Proper explanation means: define the concept, justify it in this architecture, show the idiomatic implementation, warn about failures, cite real files in the repo.
topics.why: One-line answers train nobody and cause assistants to hallucinate APIs. Operator and Hostess 7 both need reproducible reasoning on the live stack.
topics.how: Match keywords → load topic from hostess7-programming-explain.json → emit What / Why / How / Pitfalls / Where / Example. Route Command coding questions to hostess7_programming engine before generic fallback.
topics.pitfalls: Pasting generic Python tutorials; citing functions that do not exist in NEXUS; skipping why meld/brain guard matter for the answer.
topics.example: Ask: 'How does atomic panel write work here?' — get six sections with field-plate-meld citation.
